name: Cyclone Chili
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Beef chuck, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
12 Canned (or fresh) serrano peppers, seeded and chopped
1 Chopped cilantro or 12 tiny green chili leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat lard in a large heavy kettle over medium heat.
2. Add meat, about one pound at a time, and brown each portion until well-colored.
3. Once all the meat is browned, add chopped onions and garlic to the kettle. Cook until softened, stirring occasionally.
4. Return the browned beef to the kettle.
5. Rinse nopalitos in cold water, then drain and add them to the pot along with the green peppers, chopped serrano peppers, tomato paste, beef stock (or broth), coriander, cumin, salt, and pepper.
6. Stir to combine all ingredients.
7. Cover the pot and simmer over low heat for 2½ hours, stirring occasionally.
8. If using fresh tomatillos, increase the amount of beef stock or broth to 3 cups before adding them.
9. The dish is ready when it has infused with flavors; serve warm.
Note: Traditionally enjoyed by cowboys after a blustery day, this hearty chili is perfect for warming up on chilly nights.
